name: Diagnostic CAPL Functions
title: Diagnostic CAPL Functions
description: APIs for Controlling a Diagnostic Channel (K-Line)
Finds the value of a numeric communication parameter of the interface that was selected for the description in the configuration dialog.
The following callback functions are also called when the DoIP.DLL is NOT configured in the CAPL node:
The procedures are sub-divided into different events; the first matching event procedure (top-down) is called.
To use the CAPL callback interface, you have to implement the following CAPL functions:
Classes and Objects in CAPL | Declaration and Initialization of Diagnostic Objects | DoIP - Diagnostics over IP

Retrieves the position of a parameter in its primitive.
The behavior of this CAPL function depends on the used parameters. Retrieve the numeric sub-parameter from a parameter iteration. Retrieve the symbolic value of a complex parameter.
Reads/sets the complex parameter to the specified raw value.

The behavior of this CAPL function depends on the used parameters. Retrieve the value of a numeric complex parameter. Retrieve the symbolic value of a complex parameter. Retrieve the numeric sub-parameter from a parameter iteration directly.
Read the complex parameter to the specified raw value.
Returns the number of sub-parameter iterations the complex parameter holds.
The behavior of this CAPL function depends on the used parameters. Retrieve the value of the numeric parameter. Retrieve the symbolic value of the parameter. Retrieve the numeric parameter directly.
Specifies the value of a parameter directly via coded data bytes.
Copies the long name of a diagnostic parameter into the buffer, honoring the character encoding configured in the diagnostic description.
Writes the qualifier (without parent path) of the diagnostic parameter into the given field.
Returns the full qualifier path of the parameter at the given position in the primitive.
Specifies the value of a (complex) parameter directly via uncoded data bytes.
Returns the length of the coded parameter.
Returns the length of the raw parameter.
Retrieve the qualifier of the parameter's type.
Writes the unit of the parameter in the transmitted field.
Returns the number of sub-parameter iterations the complex parameter holds.

The behavior of this CAPL function depends on the used parameters. Retrieves the value of the numeric parameter. Retrieves the symbolic value of the parameter. Retrieves the numeric parameter directly.
Return the qualifier path of the parameter at the given position in the primitive.
Offers access to parameters contained in a received response object.
Retrieve the qualifier of the parameter's type.

Returns <> 0 if the parameter in the object has its default value.
Returns 1 if the parameter is declared constant in the diagnostic description.
Access/check a response parameter for a given request. Returns <> 0 if the parameter in the response has its default value.

Sets the parameter to its default value.
Sets one of the sub-parameters within a complex parameter to the specified (numeric or symbolic) value.
Sets the complex parameter to the specified raw value.
The behavior of this CAPL function depends on the used parameters. Set the numeric parameter to the specified value. Set a parameter to the symbolically-specified value.
Sets the value of a parameter directly via coded data bytes.
Sets the value of a (complex) parameter directly via uncoded data bytes.
Returns a bit mask where each bit represents a target group.
Saves the last response received (for the specified request) in the output object.
Returns the code of the last received response (for the specified request).

Delivers the qualifier path of the object that must be specified upon generation.

Polls the "suppressPosRspMsgIndicationBit" ("suppress positive response message indication bit").
Returns the name of the diagnostic target group.
Returns the qualifier of the diagnostic target group.
Reinitializes the object for the given service or primitive.
Treats the data of the response object as the specified primitive.
Treats the data of the request's response as the specified primitive.

Note With the DoIP CAPL API you can simulate a DoIP node or DoIP gateway in CAPL. For the implementation of a (DoIP) tester the following functions are not stringently necessary.
The following callback functions are also called when the DoIP.DLL is NOT configured in the CAPL node:
The TCP connection to the peer has been closed, i.e. another exchange of diagnostic messages will require a connection setup.
The tester has received a Vehicle Announcement Response from an ECU, with the given values.
The TCP communications channel to the vehicle has been established successfully, i.e. diagnostic messages can be exchanged without further delays.
The vehicle identification phase has completed, i.e. no further Vehicle Identification Response Messages are expected.
Select a specific vehicle by setting a MAC/VIN.
Configures the value of the ISO 13400-2 parameter routing activation request activation type sent by the tester the next time it starts to communicate with a vehicle on the build-in channel.
Waits until DoIP Activation Line start-up time has passed.
Note The following DoIP CAPL functions are only available with the modeling library DoIP.DLL. Information about the DoIP DLL you find on this page: DoIP CAPL Introduction
Sets the next return code sent in the acknowledgment for a diagnostic message received.
The tester received a response for an Entity Status request.
Is called if a vehicle simulation has received a Vehicle Identification Request Message.
A positive or negative acknowledgment was received in the tester.
Is called if the tester received a response for a Power Mode Information Request.
A request for routing activation was received in a vehicle simulation.
Is called if a DoIP PDU was received that cannot be interpreted by this implementation.
The DoIP implementation is about to send a TCP DoIP PDU with the given content.
Is called if an UDP packet was received that cannot be interpreted by this DoIP implementation.
An UDP packet was received. Every DoIP UDP packet is forwarded to this function. The return value determines if the packet is processed or ignored.
The DoIP implementation is about to send an UDP DoIP packet with the given content.
Is called from the DoIP layer when data was sent.
Is called from the DoIP layer when new data is received.
Is called from the DoIP layer when an error occurred.
Append another PDU to the combination.
Adds a valid DoIP ECU address to the DoIP layer.
Adds a valid DoIP tester address to the DoIP layer.
Closes the TCP connection to the peer.
Request the transfer of the given data to the DoIP peer.
Enables secure communication (TLS) for DoIP tester or ECU simulation.
Forces local TCP send port to a specific value.
Forces local UDP send port to a specific value.
Returns the last negative response code received from the peer.
Send PDU combination on open TCP connection.
Sets target IP address for broadcast messages.
Sets local IP address to bind to.
IP version of local address to select if no address is given.
Sets protocol version used in DoIP PDUs.
Sets the network interface to be used by the DoIP layer.
Sets logical DoIP address of the tester used for sending.
Sets the UDP port to be used by the DoIP layer.
Sets the network interface to be used by the DoIP layer.
Sets the address to be used by the DoIP layer.
Sets vehicle logical DoIP address sent in DoIP PDUs.
Sets the TCP port to be used by the DoIP layer.
Sets the UDP port to be used by the DoIP layer.
Sets the amount of messages written by the DoIP implementation.
Sends a DoIP PDU with valid layout on the open TCP connection.
Sends given raw data to peer(s) as UDP frame. Sends a DoIP PDU with valid layout to peer(s) as UDP frame.
(raw): Sends given raw data to peer(s) as UDP frame.(PDU): Sends given DoIP PDU to peer(s) as UDP frame.

Is called when a diagnostic request is received in the ECU simulation.
Is called when a diagnostic request has been sent by the tester.
Is called when a diagnostic response is received in the tester.
Indicates the transmission of an ISO TP data packet on a CAN channel where the ISO TP Observer is configured to supervise the communication.

Functions for Generic Diagnostics TP Interface
To use the CAPL callback interface, you have to implement the following CAPL functions:
Will be called when a diagnostic request has to be sent to a new target.
With this function CANoe triggers the CAPL interface to transmit data.
The diagnostic communication channel to the target with the given qualifier shall be closed.
The given bytes shall be sent as a functional diagnostic request to the ECU or functional group with the given qualifier.
The given bytes shall be sent as a physical diagnostic request to the ECU or functional group with the given qualifier.
With this function CANoe can be triggered to send a functional request via the CAPL interface.
This function will be called after measurement start (in ECU simulations) or after DiagSetTarget() (in test nodes) and enables the CAPL program to configure the transport protocol.
With this function the CAPL program of a tester implementation will be requested to open a channel to the ECU.
With this function the CAPL program of a tester implementation will be requested to open a channel to the ECU.
Communicates to CANoe that the communication channel is not longer available.
Tells the diagnostic layer, via the count parameter, how many bytes of data were transmitted successfully.
Passes count bytes of the transmitter address sender from the rxBuffer buffer to the diagnostic layer.
Reports errors to the diagnostic layer.
Indicates the start of a diagnostic data reception to the diagnostic layer.
Can be called if the diagnostic communication layer detected a timeout.
Configures the segmentation of diagnostic data at the transmission.
Communicates to CANoe, that a communication channel is available to the communication partner and data can be sent.
Returns the value of the "address extension" byte.
Determines the CAN ID on which functional requests are sent by the diagnostic tester.
Determines the CAN ID mask in order to be able to filter out CAN messages sent by the diagnostic tester as functional requests.
Indicates the result of the diagnostics authentication process started with diagStartAuth/diagStartAuthGeneric.
Indicates the result of the security key computation started with DiagStartGenerateKeyFromSeed.
Creates a key to execute secured diagnostic functions within devices.
Starts the diagnostics authentication process generic.
Starts generation of the security key from the seed using a Seed & Key DLL.
Checks if the response is a valid (positive or negative) response for the request.
Checks if the given negative response code is defined for the object.
Checks if the given object matches its specification in the diagnostic description.
Checks if the response received for the request matches its specification in the diagnostic description.
Sends diagnostic requests to the currently selected diagnostic target and writes the responses to the report file.
Adds an event to the set of joined events. Events: Arrival of a diagnostic response from a specific or any ECU.
Adds an event to the set of joined events that will fire when the variant identification started for the given ECU is completed.
Writes a test step with a textual interpretation of the specified request or response object into the test report.
Writes a test step with a textual interpretation of the received response for the specified request object into the test report.
Initiates the diagnostics authentication process and waits until this process has completed.
Initiates the diagnostics authentication process and waits until this process has generic completed.
Initiates the diagnostics authentication process and waits until this process has completed.
Initiates the diagnostics authentication process and waits until this process has generic completed.
Changes the active variant for the current target.
Waits for the completion of the automatic variant identification algorithm.
Waits until the previously sent request has been sent to the ECU.
Waits for arrival of the response to the given request.
Waits for the arrival of the response to a sent request, e.g. the so-called "First Frame" in ISO TP transmissions.
Performs a variant identification and activates the found variant.
Waits for the completion of the automatic variant identification algorithm.
Waits until DoIP Activation Line start-up time has passed.
Generates the security key from the seed using the configured Seed and Key DLL.
Retrieves the qualifier of the variant that is configured for the current or given target in the Diagnostics configuration dialog.

Retrieves the qualifier of the variant that has been identified by the last successful run of the automatic variant identification algorithm for given target, or the current target if no target is given.
Starts the automatic variant identification algorithm for the given target, or the currently selected one if none is given.
Changes the active variant for the current target.
Performs a variant identification and activates the found variant.
Waits for the completion of the automatic variant identification algorithm.
